other_observers: Cathy Sheeter, David Dowell, who agreed with ID. We actually saw this bird several times during the 45 minutes we followed flock around, so all of us got repeated looks, but only one photo-op;
specific_location: N Sterling Reservoir, s boat ramp
description: By this date, most Common Redpolls had gotten rather darker than they were in January. This bird stood out as almost ghostly in comparison. The background color to the upperparts was a rather pale buff. The auriculars looked white in field, perhaps with faint buff wash in photos. The underparts were white, with streaking lighter than the male COREs. The bill was short and stubby, the head big and "buffled" accentuating the smallness of the bill. The black chin was limited and a bit pale. The red poll quite small. The undertail coverts bore a single long thin streak. The rump had an unstreaked patch of moderate size and the bird scored 12 by Sibley's scale per my reckoning.Behaviors: Feeding on ground with other redpolls. Perching up in low vegetationCall: not heard, specifically, but calls like those uttered by Willie's bird were heard from this flock.Plumage: undetermined
photographer: Photographer: Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow, Steven Mlodinow
